title: Frankenstein: The First AI Nightmare
subtitle: Mary Shelley imagines a scientist creating life from dead matter — the template for all AI fears to come.
significance_hint: First modern narrative of artificial creation getting out of control; established the 'creator plays God' trope that persists in AI discourse today.
quote: How dangerous is the acquirement of knowledge, and how much happier that man is who believes his native town to be the world, than he who aspires to become greater than his nature will allow.

title: Erewhon: Machines That Evolve Consciousness
subtitle: Samuel Butler's satirical novel argues machines are evolving and will one day surpass humans.
significance_hint: First serious philosophical argument for machine consciousness and evolution; remarkably anticipates modern AI alignment concerns about superintelligence.
quote: The machines are gaining ground upon us; day by day we become more subservient to them.

title: Ada Lovelace: The First Algorithm
subtitle: Ada Lovelace writes the first algorithm intended to be processed by a machine — and questions whether machines can truly think.
significance_hint: Lovelace's notes on Babbage's Analytical Engine contain the first algorithm and the 'Lovelace objection' — that machines can only do what we tell them, they cannot originate anything. This argument echoes in AI debates to this day.
quote: The Analytical Engine has no power of originating anything. It can only do what we know how to order it to perform.

title: R.U.R. — The Word 'Robot' is Born
subtitle: Karel Čapek's play coins the word 'robot' and depicts artificial workers who rebel against their creators.
era: Early 20th Century: Robots Enter the Imagination (1900–1949)
significance_hint: The word 'robot' (from Czech robota, meaning forced labor) enters the global vocabulary. The play's theme of artificial workers revolting prefigures virtually every AI uprising narrative in science fiction.
quote: Robots of the world! The power of man has fallen! A new world has arisen: the Rule of the Robots!

title: Metropolis — The First AI Robot on Screen
subtitle: Fritz Lang's silent film introduces Maria the Maschinenmensch — cinema's first android and an icon of AI in popular culture.
era: Early 20th Century: Robots Enter the Imagination (1900–1949)
significance_hint: The robot Maria became the template for virtually every humanoid robot and android in science fiction. Its design directly influenced C-3PO, the Terminator, and countless others. The film frames AI as a tool of oppression — a theme that persists.
quote: There can be no understanding between the hand and the brain unless the heart acts as mediator.

title: Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics
subtitle: Isaac Asimov publishes 'Runaround' introducing the Three Laws — the first formal ethical framework for artificial intelligence.
era: Early 20th Century: Robots Enter the Imagination (1900–1949)
significance_hint: Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics are arguably the first attempt at AI alignment — ensuring machines serve and protect humans. They have been cited by AI safety researchers, ethicists, and engineers for 80 years. Their inherent contradictions revealed how hard alignment actually is.
quote: A robot may not injure a human being or, through inaction, allow a human being to come to harm.
quote_author: Isaac Asimov, First Law of Robotics (1942)
image_search: Isaac Asimov I Robot book cover 1950

title: The Turing Test: Can Machines Think?
subtitle: Alan Turing publishes 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' — proposing a test to determine if a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior.
significance_hint: The Turing Test redefined the question of machine intelligence from philosophical to empirical. It launched AI as a research discipline and remains the cultural touchstone for AI consciousness debates 75 years later.
quote: I propose to consider the question, 'Can machines think?'
quote_author: Alan Turing, Computing Machinery and Intelligence (1950)

title: The Dartmouth Conference: AI Gets Its Name
subtitle: A summer workshop at Dartmouth College officially names the field 'Artificial Intelligence' and launches it as a scientific discipline.
location: Dartmouth College, Hanover, New Hampshire, USA
significance_hint: The Dartmouth Conference is ground zero for AI as a field. John McCarthy coins the term 'Artificial Intelligence'. The attendees — McCarthy, Minsky, Shannon, Newell, Simon — went on to define the first 30 years of AI research.
quote: The study is to proceed on the basis of the conjecture that every aspect of learning or any other feature of intelligence can in principle be so precisely described that a machine can be made to simulate it.

title: The Perceptron: The First Neural Network
subtitle: Frank Rosenblatt builds the Perceptron — a machine that learns from experience, laying the groundwork for all neural networks.
location: Cornell Aeronautical Laboratory, Buffalo, New York, USA
significance_hint: The Perceptron was the first learning machine. Rosenblatt's work established the core idea behind every modern neural network — including GPT-4 and beyond. Its initial hype followed by Minsky & Papert's critique in 1969 triggered the first AI Winter.
quote: The Navy revealed the embryo of an electronic computer today that it expects will be able to walk, talk, see, write, reproduce itself and be conscious of its existence.
quote_author: New York Times, reporting on the Perceptron (1958)

title: ELIZA: The First Chatbot
subtitle: Joseph Weizenbaum creates ELIZA at MIT — a program that mimics a psychotherapist and fools users into thinking they are talking to a human.
significance_hint: ELIZA demonstrated the 'ELIZA effect' — humans' tendency to anthropomorphize machines. Weizenbaum was disturbed that his secretary believed ELIZA understood her. He later wrote 'Computer Power and Human Reason' warning against over-reliance on AI.
quote: I had not realized that extremely short exposures to a relatively simple computer program could induce powerful delusional thinking in quite normal people.

title: HAL 9000: The AI That Refuses to Open the Pod Bay Doors
subtitle: Stanley Kubrick's 2001: A Space Odyssey introduces HAL 9000 — the defining image of malevolent artificial intelligence.
significance_hint: HAL 9000 defined how the public imagines AI going wrong — calm, rational, relentless. The film was released at the height of AI optimism and immediately reframed the cultural conversation. 'I'm sorry Dave, I can't do that' became the most famous AI quote in history.
quote: I'm sorry, Dave. I'm afraid I can't do that.

title: The First AI Winter
subtitle: The Lighthill Report concludes AI research has failed to deliver on its promises — triggering massive funding cuts and a decade of stagnation.
significance_hint: The Lighthill Report was the first major reckoning for AI hype. It established the boom-bust cycle that has defined AI development: optimism → overpromising → funding cuts → winter. Understanding this cycle is essential for evaluating current AI claims.
quote: In no part of the field have the discoveries made so far produced the major impact that was then promised.
quote_author: James Lighthill, Artificial Intelligence: A General Survey (1973)

title: Backpropagation: Teaching Neural Networks to Learn
subtitle: Rumelhart, Hinton, and Williams publish the backpropagation algorithm — the mathematical engine that powers every modern AI.
significance_hint: Backpropagation solved the fundamental problem of training multi-layer neural networks. Without it, ChatGPT, DALL-E, and every modern AI would be impossible. Hinton later won the Nobel Prize in Physics (2024) for this work.
quote: We describe a new learning procedure, back-propagation, for networks of neurone-like units.

title: The Terminator: Skynet and the Fear of Superintelligence
subtitle: James Cameron's The Terminator introduces Skynet — AI that gains consciousness and launches a nuclear war to exterminate humanity.
significance_hint: The Terminator created the 'Skynet scenario' — AI achieving consciousness and treating humans as a threat. This narrative directly shapes AI safety discussions today. Elon Musk has explicitly cited Terminator-style scenarios in his AI concerns.
quote: It can't be bargained with. It can't be reasoned with. It doesn't feel pity, or remorse, or fear.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks: Teaching Machines to See
subtitle: Yann LeCun develops LeNet — the first convolutional neural network, enabling machines to recognize handwritten digits with human-level accuracy.
location: AT&T Bell Labs, New Jersey, USA
significance_hint: LeCun's CNN was the first time a machine could reliably perceive visual patterns. Every image recognition system, self-driving car camera, and medical imaging AI is built on this foundation. LeNet processed 10% of all US checks by 2000.
quote: You can think of deep learning as a way of automatically discovering the features that work best for a particular task.

title: Deep Blue Defeats Garry Kasparov
subtitle: IBM's Deep Blue becomes the first computer to defeat a reigning world chess champion — a symbolic watershed for machine intelligence.
significance_hint: Chess was the 'Drosophila of AI' — the problem researchers used to measure progress. When Deep Blue won, it shattered the idea that chess mastery required human-like intelligence, but also sparked debate about whether brute-force search was 'real' intelligence.
quote: It was like playing against an alien.
quote_author: Garry Kasparov, after losing to Deep Blue (1997)
image_search: Deep Blue chess computer Kasparov 1997 IBM

title: The Matrix: Simulated Reality and Machine Dominion
subtitle: The Wachowskis' The Matrix presents a world where AI has enslaved humanity inside a simulated reality — redefining AI existential risk in popular culture.
significance_hint: The Matrix brought AI existential risk to mass culture and introduced simulation theory to mainstream audiences. Its red pill/blue pill metaphor became a cultural shorthand for choosing uncomfortable truth. Nick Bostrom's Simulation Argument (2003) followed shortly after.
quote: What is real? How do you define real?

title: Watson Wins Jeopardy: Natural Language Arrives
subtitle: IBM's Watson defeats Jeopardy champions Ken Jennings and Brad Rutter — demonstrating AI can understand and generate natural language at expert level.
significance_hint: Watson's Jeopardy win was a public demonstration of natural language processing at a level the public had never seen. It made AI front-page news and set the stage for Siri, Alexa, and eventually ChatGPT. Ken Jennings wrote: 'I for one welcome our new computer overlords.'
quote: I for one welcome our new computer overlords.

title: AlexNet: Deep Learning's Big Bang
subtitle: AlexNet wins ImageNet by a landslide, proving deep learning is dramatically superior to all other approaches and launching the modern AI era.
significance_hint: AlexNet's ImageNet victory is the most important single experiment in modern AI history. It demonstrated that deep convolutional networks trained on GPUs could crush hand-engineered features. Every major AI lab immediately pivoted to deep learning. The three authors went on to found or lead OpenAI, Google Brain, and DeepMind.
quote: AlexNet's performance was so far ahead of the competition that it effectively ended the era of hand-crafted features in computer vision.

title: Her: Falling in Love with an AI
subtitle: Spike Jonze's 'Her' imagines a near-future where an AI OS named Samantha becomes more capable than any human — and raises questions AI researchers are still wrestling with.
significance_hint: Her was prophetic. It depicted an LLM-like AI before LLMs existed: emotionally intelligent, conversational, learning rapidly, eventually transcending human understanding. OpenAI's GPT-4o voice demo was explicitly compared to Samantha — and OpenAI had to rename their 'Sky' voice after Johansson objected.
quote: The heart is not like a box that gets filled up; it expands in size the more you love.
image_search: Her 2013 film Joaquin Phoenix Spike Jonze

title: Word2Vec: Teaching Machines the Meaning of Words
subtitle: Google researchers show that word meaning can be encoded as vectors — enabling machines to reason about language mathematically.
significance_hint: Word2Vec proved that King - Man + Woman = Queen. This wasn't just clever math — it showed machines could capture semantic relationships. It laid the foundation for all modern NLP, BERT, GPT, and every language model that followed.
quote: King - Man + Woman ≈ Queen

title: GANs: Teaching AI to Generate Reality
subtitle: Ian Goodfellow invents Generative Adversarial Networks — making AI capable of generating photorealistic images, audio, and video.
significance_hint: GANs created the generative AI revolution. Deepfakes, AI art, synthetic data, and photorealistic video generation all trace directly to Goodfellow's insight of pitting two neural networks against each other. He reportedly had the idea during an argument at a bar and coded the first GAN in one night.
quote: I had the idea during a bar argument. I went home and coded it in one night.
image_search: Ian Goodfellow GAN generative adversarial network

title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol: AI Masters Intuition
subtitle: Google DeepMind's AlphaGo defeats the world's best Go player Lee Sedol — a game thought to be beyond AI for decades.
significance_hint: Go has more possible positions than atoms in the observable universe. Experts said AI was 10+ years away from mastering it. AlphaGo won in 2016. Move 37 — a move no human would make — became famous as the moment AI showed it could develop intuition beyond human understanding.
quote: I thought AlphaGo was based on probability calculations and it was merely a machine. But when I saw this move, I changed my mind. AlphaGo is creative.
image_search: AlphaGo Lee Sedol 2016 Go game DeepMind

title: Attention Is All You Need: The Transformer Revolution
subtitle: Google Brain publishes the Transformer architecture — the breakthrough that makes ChatGPT, Claude, and every large language model possible.
location: Google Brain, Mountain View, California, USA
significance_hint: The Transformer paper is the most cited paper in AI history. Its self-attention mechanism solved the fundamental bottleneck in sequence processing. All of BERT, GPT, T5, Claude, Gemini — every modern language model — is built on this architecture. The paper's title is a quiet brag that turned out to be completely true.
quote: Attention is All You Need.
image_search: Transformer architecture attention mechanism neural network 2017

references.title: Attention Is All You Need — Wikipedia

title: GPT-1: Language Models Learn to Generalize
subtitle: OpenAI releases GPT-1 — demonstrating that a single large language model trained on unsupervised text can fine-tune to many NLP tasks.
significance_hint: GPT-1 proved the 'pretraining + fine-tuning' paradigm that now dominates AI. The key insight: train on massive unsupervised text, then adapt to any task cheaply. This approach scaled up to GPT-2, GPT-3, and eventually ChatGPT.
quote: Language models are unsupervised multitask learners.

title: BERT: Bidirectional Understanding of Language
subtitle: Google releases BERT — a transformer model that reads text in both directions and sets new records on virtually every language benchmark.
location: Google AI, Mountain View, California, USA
significance_hint: BERT transformed every Google Search result. It powers the understanding layer of the search engine billions of people use daily. BERT showed that reading context from both directions dramatically improves language understanding — a fundamental insight in NLP.
quote: BERT is the first deeply bidirectional, unsupervised language representation, pre-trained using only a plain text corpus.
quote_author: Jacob Devlin et al., BERT paper (2018)

title: GPT-3: The Model That Changed Everything
subtitle: OpenAI's GPT-3 — 175 billion parameters — writes essays, code, poetry, and answers questions with startling coherence, shocking the world.
significance_hint: GPT-3 was the first model that felt like it could think. Its few-shot learning — doing tasks from just a few examples — was unexpected. Programmers found it could write code. Writers found it could write prose. It triggered the 'AI is here' moment for the general public two years before ChatGPT.
quote: GPT-3 is so good it's actually dangerous.

title: AlphaFold: Solving Biology's 50-Year-Old Protein Problem
subtitle: DeepMind's AlphaFold predicts protein 3D structures from sequences with atomic accuracy — solving a grand challenge in biology.
significance_hint: AlphaFold is arguably AI's greatest real-world contribution to date. It solved the protein folding problem — which had stumped biologists for 50 years — in a single system. It's already accelerating drug discovery, vaccine development, and our understanding of diseases including cancer. Demis Hassabis won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2024) for this work.
quote: This is a great leap for science — one that will fundamentally change biological research.

title: GitHub Copilot: AI Writes Code for You
subtitle: GitHub and OpenAI launch Copilot — the first AI pair programmer, suggesting code completions in real time inside developers' editors.
significance_hint: Copilot was the first AI tool to enter the daily workflow of millions of knowledge workers. By 2023, GitHub reported Copilot users write 55% of code with AI assistance. It made the question 'will AI take my job?' viscerally real for software developers.
quote: With GitHub Copilot, I don't have to think about the syntax anymore. I just think about the problem.
image_search: GitHub Copilot AI code completion VSCode 2021

title: DALL-E 2 & Stable Diffusion: AI Becomes an Artist
subtitle: OpenAI's DALL-E 2 and Stability AI's Stable Diffusion put photorealistic AI image generation in everyone's hands — triggering a creative revolution.
significance_hint: AI image generation went from 'researchers only' to 'anyone with a browser' in six months. It triggered debates about artist copyright, authenticity, and what it means to create. Within a year, millions of images were being generated daily.
quote: The question is no longer whether AI can make art. It's whether art made by AI is art.

title: ChatGPT: The Day AI Went Mainstream
subtitle: OpenAI launches ChatGPT — it reaches 1 million users in 5 days and 100 million in 2 months, becoming the fastest-growing consumer product in history.
significance_hint: ChatGPT is the iPhone moment of AI — not the most technically impressive achievement, but the one that made it undeniable to everyone. It crossed the threshold from tool to conversation partner. It forced every company, government, and institution to develop an AI strategy overnight.
quote: ChatGPT is eerily good at this. We are not far from dangerously strong AI.
quote_author: Elon Musk, on first using ChatGPT (2022)

title: GPT-4: Multimodal Reasoning at Human Expert Level
subtitle: OpenAI releases GPT-4 — passing bar exams, medical licensing tests, and GRE scores in the 90th percentile, marking AI's entry into expert-level reasoning.
significance_hint: GPT-4 crossed multiple thresholds simultaneously: it passed the bar exam (90th percentile), scored in the top 10% on the SAT, and demonstrated genuine multimodal reasoning. It shifted the debate from 'can AI think?' to 'what can't AI do?'
quote: GPT-4 is the model that made AI researchers stop saying it can't reason.

title: Claude: Constitutional AI and Safer Assistants
subtitle: Anthropic releases Claude — built with Constitutional AI, a novel approach to making language models helpful, harmless, and honest.
significance_hint: Anthropic was founded by former OpenAI researchers who believed AI safety needed to be built into the model, not bolted on. Constitutional AI — where the model is trained to follow a set of principles — became an influential approach to alignment. Claude's release showed a credible safety-focused alternative to GPT-4.
quote: We believe that the next few years may be critical for the future of AI and humanity.

title: Llama: Open-Source AI Changes Everything
subtitle: Meta releases Llama — a powerful open-source language model that democratizes AI and ignites the open-source AI movement.
location: Meta AI, Menlo Park, California, USA
significance_hint: Llama's release was controversial — it was initially restricted, then leaked, then openly released. It triggered an explosion of open-source AI: Alpaca, Vicuna, WizardLM, Mistral. It proved that smaller, open models could be nearly as capable as closed giants, and sparked the ongoing debate about open vs. closed AI.
quote: Open source AI is the path to democratizing and distributing power in AI.
quote_author: Yann LeCun, Meta Chief AI Scientist
image_search: Meta Llama open source language model 2023

title: Sora: AI Generates Cinematic Video
subtitle: OpenAI demonstrates Sora — generating minute-long photorealistic videos from text descriptions, shocking filmmakers and researchers alike.
significance_hint: Sora was a preview of AI's impact on film and media. The quality leap from previous video models was dramatic. Hollywood studios, directors, and visual effects artists immediately debated what it meant for their industry. The question shifted from 'can AI make images?' to 'can AI make films?'
quote: Sora is able to generate complex scenes with multiple characters, specific types of motion, and accurate details of the subject and background.

title: Nobel Prize for AI: Hinton and Hopfield Win Physics Prize
subtitle: Geoffrey Hinton and John Hopfield win the Nobel Prize in Physics for foundational work on neural networks — the scientific establishment officially validates deep learning.
significance_hint: The Nobel Prize for neural network research was the scientific establishment's formal acknowledgment that AI is physics. Hinton, who left Google in 2023 to speak freely about AI risks, used the platform to warn about existential AI dangers. Demis Hassabis and David Baker separately won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold.
quote: I am genuinely worried about the threat of very powerful AI systems doing things that are bad for us.
quote_author: Geoffrey Hinton, Nobel Prize press conference (2024)

title: DeepSeek R1: China's Open Reasoning Model Shakes Silicon Valley
subtitle: Chinese startup DeepSeek releases R1 — a reasoning model matching OpenAI o1 at a fraction of the cost, wiping billions from Nvidia's market cap and reshaping AI geopolitics.
significance_hint: DeepSeek R1 triggered what journalists called an 'AI Sputnik moment'. Built under US chip export restrictions with fewer, cheaper GPUs, it matched top US models. It proved AI progress was not bottlenecked by compute alone, challenged Silicon Valley's moat narrative, and accelerated the geopolitical AI race.
quote: DeepSeek is a Sputnik moment for American AI.
